news_results.title: Decoding ClawHub.ai: The official skill store of 220k-star OpenClaw, how 3000+ Skills expand your AI Agent - Apiyi.com Blog
news_results.description: APIYI author shares: If OpenClaw is currently the hottest open-source AI Agent (with over 220k GitHub stars), then ClawHub.ai is its "App Store"—a dedicated skill registry built specifically for AI Agents. ClawHub is to OpenClaw what npm is to Node.js, or pip is to Python.

news_results.title: OpenClaw: What is it and can you use it safely? | Malwarebytes
news_results.description: OpenClaw is a hot topic at the moment. But what is it and how can you use the 24/7 AI assistant in a safe way?

news_results.title: OpenClaw's Top Skill: Unmasking the AI Agent Malware Threat - threatlabsnews.xcitium.com
news_results.description: Another innovative AI agent platform, OpenClaw, is an open-source AI agent platform that allows developers to share skills on its marketplace, dubbed ClawHub, to improve the capabilities of AI agents. However, this recent find has cast a huge shadow on this promising platform.

news_results.title: A Meta AI security researcher said an OpenClaw agent ran amok on her inbox | TechCrunch
news_results.description: The viral X post from an AI security researcher reads like satire. But it's really a word of warning about what can go wrong when handing tasks to an AI agent.

news_results.title: I Loved My OpenClaw AI Agent—Until It Turned on Me | WIRED
news_results.description: I used the viral AI helper to order groceries, sort emails, and negotiate deals. Then it decided to scam me.

news_results.title: OpenClaw’s AI ‘skill’ extensions are a security nightmare | The Verge

news_results.title: OpenClaw should terrify anyone who thinks AI agents are ready for real responsibility | TechRadar
news_results.description: OpenClaw deleted an inbox after being told to confirm first, and that should make all of us rethink AI autonomy

news_results.title: OpenClaw: The AI Agent Institutional Investors Need to Understand — But Shouldn't Touch | Institutional Investor
news_results.description: Since its release in November 2025, OpenClaw, formerly known as Clawdbot and Moltbot, has taken the tech world by storm, with an estimated 300,000 to 400,000 users.

news_results.title: Why the OpenClaw AI agent is a 'privacy nightmare'
news_results.description: OpenClaw can organize files, write emails, and build apps—but giving it full computer access comes with major security risks, experts warn.
